'Institutional evaluation' is not directly referenced, and other categories lack explicit support.
low_level_thinking.Policy theme: The paragraph emphasizes forecasting based on data and considering national income/tax yields, aligning with financing/resource mobilization. Governance/planning is addressed through mentions of bodies responsible for studying financial aspects and making forecasts. Other categories like access or quality are not directly mentioned.
low_level_thinking.Cross-cutting themes & skills: The paragraph emphasizes forecasting based on financial data (taxes, national income) and economic trends, directly aligning with 'Financial and economic literacy'. 'Other cross-cutting theme' is included as the text discusses broader systemic planning and data analysis, which fits the contested 'Cross-cutting themes & skills' category. Categories like 'Policy theme' or 'Subject domain' are excluded as the focus is on financial literacy rather than policy or specific subjects.

TITLE: LE FINANCEMENT DE L’ÉDUCATION
paragraph: 8. The findings of the bodies responsible for studying the financial aspects of education should be used notably: a) in the preparation of annual education budgets at various levels; b) in the planning of certain expenditure programs extending beyond the annual fiscal year; c) in the development of any new legislative measures concerning the modalities of education financing; d) in the preparation of any educational laws and decrees whose implementation entails a call for funds;

low_level_thinking.Legal frameworks: The paragraph explicitly mentions legislative measures and decrees (Education laws and regulations) and budget preparation/planning (Policy implementation and enforcement). 'Legal frameworks' is addressed through legislative actions, while 'Policy' is tied to financial planning. 'Constitution and fundamental rights' is excluded as no rights-based language is present.
low_level_thinking.Policy theme: The paragraph focuses on using financial study findings for budgeting, expenditure planning, legislative measures, and legal frameworks, directly aligning with 'Financing and resource mobilization'. 'Governance and planning' is supported by references to policy development and strategic financial planning mentioned in the orchestrator summary. Other categories like 'Legal frameworks' are excluded as the text emphasizes policy and financial planning over legal specifics.

TITLE: LE FINANCEMENT DE L’ÉDUCATION
paragraph: 9. The work of bodies responsible for studying the financial aspect of education, in which statistical data will play a primary role, must take into account, among other factors: a) economic factors (amount of national income, natural resource wealth, degree of industrialization, agricultural production, standard of living, possibilities for economic development); b) administrative factors (administrative structure at the national, regional, or local level, tax system, financing methods); c) demographic factors (population growth, changes in the distribution of urban and rural populations, growth in student enrollments at all levels, especially primary education in countries that have not yet succeeded in generalizing compulsory schooling); d) pedagogical factors (level of development of education concerning teaching staff, material equipment, auxiliary and extracurricular services, etc.).

low_level_thinking.Policy theme: The paragraph addresses administrative structures (governance), financial aspects (financing), and pedagogical factors (curriculum/pedagogy). 'Education system monitoring & evaluation' is excluded as the text focuses on factors influencing education systems rather than evaluation mechanisms. 'Pedagogy & assessment' is narrowed to 'Curriculum and pedagogy policies' to align with the specific mention of teaching staff and material equipment.
low_level_thinking.Education system monitoring & evaluation: The paragraph emphasizes statistical data (education statistics and indicators), data systems for financial analysis (EMIS and data systems), and administrative factors requiring institutional evaluation (institutional evaluation and inspection). 'Learning assessments' and 'quality assurance' are excluded as the focus is on systemic data collection, not individual assessments or accreditation.

TITLE: LE FINANCEMENT DE L’ÉDUCATION
paragraph: 10. Among the tasks that may be undertaken by bodies responsible for studying the financial aspect of education, some appear to be of greater urgency: coordination of the financial intervention of public authorities involved in education; possible simplification of the administrative procedures for financing and control measures; comparative study of the national financial organization in education and that of other countries; study of the sectors or aspects of education urgently requiring new funding; assessment of the probable increase in educational needs over the coming years in relation to the development of taxation during the same period.

low_level_thinking.Policy theme: The paragraph emphasizes financial coordination, administrative procedures, and comparative studies of financial systems, directly aligning with 'Financing and resource mobilization'. 'Governance and planning' is supported by mentions of coordination among public authorities and policy development. 'Education system monitoring & evaluation' from the summary is excluded as the focus is on financial aspects, not evaluation.
low_level_thinking.Education system monitoring & evaluation: The paragraph mentions comparative studies of financial systems and assessment of educational needs, aligning with 'Research and surveys'. References to financial organization and taxation relate to 'Education statistics and indicators'. 'Institutional evaluation' and 'EMIS' are excluded as the focus is on financial analysis, not institutional oversight or data systems.

TITLE: LE FINANCEMENT DE L’ÉDUCATION
paragraph: Financial Responsibilities of Public Authorities AT DIFFERENT LEVELS 11. Without expressing an opinion on the value of any particular system of education financing—a system which depends on the administrative structure of each country, itself the result of geographical, historical, political, economic, social, and other circumstances—efforts must be made to avoid any duplication and to simplify, as far as is compatible with efficiency, the allocation of financial responsibilities among central or federal public authorities, regional public authorities (states, provinces, cantons, etc. in federal-type countries; provinces, departments, etc. in other countries), and local authorities (municipal administrations or administrations grouping several municipalities into school districts);

low_level_thinking.Policy theme: The paragraph focuses on allocation of financial responsibilities among different governance levels (central, regional, local), directly aligning with 'Financing and resource mobilization'. It also discusses systemic allocation and roles across administrative structures, supporting 'Governance and planning'. 'Legal frameworks' and 'Education system monitoring & evaluation' are excluded as the text lacks legal specifics or evaluation mechanisms.
low_level_thinking.Stakeholder focus: The paragraph focuses on financial responsibilities among central, regional, and local authorities, which are governmental entities. Other categories like teachers, learners, or private sector are not mentioned. The emphasis on governance structures aligns with 'Government and policy makers' rather than legal frameworks or civil society.

TITLE: LE FINANCEMENT DE L’ÉDUCATION
paragraph: 12. In countries of vast expanse and in countries where ethnic and cultural groups are distinctly differentiated, it seems appropriate to entrust a significant portion of financial responsibilities to the public authorities constituted by the federated States, provinces, or cantons, these authorities then playing a role similar to that of the central authorities in other countries;

low_level_thinking.Policy theme: The paragraph discusses financial responsibilities entrusted to public authorities in federated systems, aligning with 'Financing and resource mobilization'. It also mentions the structure and roles of authorities, fitting 'Governance and planning'. 'Legal frameworks' and 'Stakeholder focus' were considered but not explicitly supported by the text.
low_level_thinking.Legal frameworks: The paragraph discusses financial responsibilities entrusted to public authorities in federated systems, aligning with 'Legal frameworks' as it outlines structural arrangements. 'Policy implementation and enforcement' fits due to the mention of authorities 'playing a role similar to that of the central authorities,' indicating policy execution. 'Stakeholder focus' is excluded as the text does not explicitly address stakeholder obligations or rights, but rather institutional roles.
low_level_thinking.Stakeholder focus: The paragraph emphasizes public authorities in federated structures, aligning with government and policy makers. Other categories like civil society or private sector are not mentioned. The focus on financial responsibilities and federated systems supports government role rather than broader stakeholder categories.

TITLE: LE FINANCEMENT DE L’ÉDUCATION
paragraph: 13. When deciding the proportion of education funding to be allocated to local authorities relative to that of central or federal authorities and regional authorities, consideration should be given to the level of development and vitality of local life, as well as the economic resources available to local authorities to meet the responsibilities and obligations envisaged for them; the central administration should ensure an equitable distribution of resources allocated to education within each district, so as to guarantee each of its residents the full exercise of the right to education.

low_level_thinking.Ownership/Provision: The paragraph emphasizes central administration's role in ensuring equitable resource distribution, indicating state involvement in education provision. 'Public (state-run)' aligns with this central authority responsibility. Other categories like 'Private non-profit' or 'PPP' are excluded as the text does not mention private sector involvement. 'Community or faith-based' is also excluded since the focus is on governmental resource allocation rather than community-led initiatives.
low_level_thinking.Policy theme: The paragraph discusses funding allocation between authorities, equitable resource distribution, and intergovernmental responsibilities, aligning with 'Financing and resource mobilization' and 'Access and equity'. 'Governance and planning' is relevant due to mentions of decision-making structures and distribution mechanisms. 'Infrastructure & resources' from the summary is excluded as it lacks direct mention in the paragraph.
low_level_thinking.Infrastructure & resources: The paragraph focuses on intergovernmental funding allocation and equitable resource distribution, which does not directly align with specific infrastructure types. 'Other' is the only category that can encompass the broader discussion of resource distribution and economic resources without over-labeling.

TITLE: LE FINANCEMENT DE L’ÉDUCATION
paragraph: 14. In countries where financial responsibilities are shared among public authorities at different levels, it is desirable that the financial participation of local authorities, assisted to varying degrees by higher administrative bodies (central or federal authorities and regional authorities), should primarily cover preschool education, compulsory education, and auxiliary services, which does not in any way exclude their participation (especially when it concerns local authorities of some significance) in the financing of other categories of education or expenses.

low_level_thinking.Education level: The paragraph explicitly mentions preschool education (ISCED 02) and compulsory education (ISCED 1). 'Multiple levels' is included because the text discusses varying financial responsibilities across different education levels. 'Early childhood educational development (ISCED 01)' is excluded as the paragraph specifies preschool/primary, not developmental programs. 'Secondary education' and higher tiers are not mentioned.
low_level_thinking.Ownership/Provision: The paragraph discusses financial responsibilities shared among public authorities (local, regional, central/federal), indicating public ownership. The mention of local authorities being 'assisted to varying degrees by higher administrative bodies' suggests a public-private partnership model. 'Private non-profit' and 'Private for-profit' are excluded as the text focuses on public entities. 'Community or faith-based' is not mentioned.
low_level_thinking.Stakeholder focus: The paragraph discusses financial responsibilities shared among public authorities (government/policy makers) and mentions local authorities' role, which includes community-supported education. 'Parents and communities' is implied through the mention of local authorities' participation in financing, aligning with community involvement in education funding.
low_level_thinking.Legal frameworks: The paragraph discusses financial responsibilities and participation of local authorities in education financing, aligning with 'Education laws and regulations' regarding policy frameworks. 'Rights and obligations of stakeholders' applies as it addresses the roles and responsibilities of different authorities. 'Policy implementation and enforcement' is excluded as the text focuses on normative guidance rather than enforcement mechanisms.

TITLE: LE FINANCEMENT DE L’ÉDUCATION
paragraph: 15. The aid provided by higher administrative authorities to local authorities should be based on the latter’s economic capacities; while this assistance may be proportional to the contribution of local authorities in the case of prosperous communities, it should be considerably greater in the case of disadvantaged communities.

low_level_thinking.Ownership/Provision: The paragraph discusses aid from higher administrative authorities to local authorities, indicating a state-run provision model. 'Public (state-run)' directly aligns with this top-down resource allocation. Other categories like 'Private non-profit' or 'Community-based' are excluded as the text emphasizes governmental entities, not private or community-led initiatives.

TITLE: LE FINANCEMENT DE L’ÉDUCATION
paragraph: 16. The control exercised by the financial authorities, at various levels, over the different categories of educational institutions or activities they fund must be rationalized and simplified to the greatest extent possible and must not constitute an obstacle to the functioning, efficiency, and development of educational work.

low_level_thinking.Ownership/Provision: The paragraph emphasizes financial authorities' control over educational institutions, implying state involvement. 'Public' aligns with state-run institutions. Other categories like private or PPP are not explicitly mentioned. The focus on simplifying control suggests existing public oversight rather than new partnerships or private entities.
low_level_thinking.Policy theme: The paragraph focuses on simplifying financial control and governance structures, aligning with 'Governance and planning'. 'Accountability and transparency' is relevant due to the emphasis on rationalizing control to ensure efficient functioning. Other categories like 'Financing and resource mobilization' are partially related but not as directly supported.

TITLE: LE FINANCEMENT DE L’ÉDUCATION
paragraph: — 129 — Financing of the various levels of education 17. Although public authorities generally attribute only relative importance to preschool education, it is necessary to draw their attention to the increasingly greater needs they will have to face in this area, without neglecting the rights and duties of the family;

low_level_thinking.Policy theme: The paragraph directly addresses financing of education levels and mentions the need to draw public authorities' attention to financial needs, aligning with 'Financing and resource mobilization'. It also references the role of public authorities and family responsibilities, fitting 'Governance and planning'. Other categories like 'Access and equity' or 'Inclusion and diversity' are not explicitly mentioned.
low_level_thinking.Stakeholder focus: The paragraph mentions 'public authorities' and the need to 'draw their attention' to preschool education needs, aligning with Government and policy makers. It also references the 'rights and duties of the family', directly linking to Parents and communities. Other categories like Teachers or Civil society are not explicitly mentioned.
low_level_thinking.Education level: The paragraph explicitly mentions preschool education and references rights and duties of the family, aligning with ISCED 01 and 02. 'Early childhood care and education unspecified (ISCED 0)' is excluded as the text specifies preschool education, not general early childhood care.

TITLE: LE FINANCEMENT DE L’ÉDUCATION
paragraph: 18. In countries where the universalization of compulsory primary education for both sexes has not yet been achieved, priority should be given to the investment and operational expenditures required for this universalization, in order to ensure its implementation as rapidly as possible;

low_level_thinking.Policy theme: The paragraph emphasizes universal primary education for both sexes (access and equity), prioritizes investment and operational expenditures (financing), and implies governmental responsibility for implementation (governance). 'Infrastructure & resources' was excluded as the focus is on funding priorities, not physical infrastructure.
low_level_thinking.Ownership/Provision: The paragraph emphasizes governmental responsibility ('priority should be given to the investment...') indicating public ownership. 'Infrastructure & resources' is supported by mentions of investment and operational expenditures. 'Private' categories are excluded as the text focuses on state-led priorities without mentioning private entities.

TITLE: LE FINANCEMENT DE L’ÉDUCATION
paragraph: 19. It is extremely urgent to draw the attention of the authorities in all countries to the financial implications of the increasingly strong movement advocating equal access to secondary education, regardless of race, sex, religion, or social status; indeed, all indications suggest that, within a timeframe that will vary according to the country, secondary school enrollments will progressively approach those of primary education.

low_level_thinking.Education level: The paragraph explicitly mentions 'secondary education' and discusses enrollment trends approaching primary education, aligning with ISCED 2 and 3. Other categories like primary or tertiary are excluded as the focus is on secondary access, not early childhood or higher education.
low_level_thinking.Learner population: The paragraph emphasizes equal access to secondary education regardless of race, sex, religion, or social status, aligning with 'All learners'. The financial implications and varying timelines suggest focus on low-income/disadvantaged groups. Other categories like 'Women and girls' or 'Indigenous groups' are not explicitly mentioned.
low_level_thinking.Policy theme: The paragraph emphasizes equal access to secondary education across demographics (Access and equity) and highlights financial implications of achieving this (Financing and resource mobilization). Governance and planning is excluded as the focus is on financial aspects rather than institutional structures.

TITLE: LE FINANCEMENT DE L’ÉDUCATION
paragraph: 20. Given its quantitative and qualitative development (increasingly advanced professional specialization) and the high cost of equipment, all indications are that the importance of expenditures for technical and vocational education will only increase; considering the still rather fluid structure of this education in certain countries, it is time to consider: a) coordination of the structure of vocational and technical education with that of primary, secondary, and higher education; b) rationalization of financial efforts made in this field by central or federal authorities, by regional authorities, and by local authorities; c) rationalization of financial efforts made in this field by ministries or departments of public education and by other ministries or administrative services; d) strengthening voluntary support that can be expected from private initiative, which, thanks to the development of technical and vocational education, should increasingly bear less of the burden of apprenticeship.

low_level_thinking.Policy theme: The paragraph emphasizes coordination of education structures (Governance and planning), rationalization of financial efforts (Financing and resource mobilization), and mentions high costs of equipment and technical specialization (Innovation and technology policies). Other categories like curriculum or access are not explicitly addressed.
low_level_thinking.Education level: The paragraph focuses on technical and vocational education, which aligns with ISCED 4 (post-secondary non-tertiary). The mention of coordination across primary, secondary, and higher education, along with financial rationalization across multiple levels of governance, supports 'Multiple levels' as a complementary category.
low_level_thinking.Ownership/Provision: The paragraph mentions coordination of education structures (implying public involvement), rationalization of financial efforts by central/federal/ regional/local authorities (public sector), and voluntary support from private initiative (private non-profit). PPP is suggested by the call for private support alongside public coordination. Private for-profit is excluded as the private sector's role is framed as voluntary support, not profit-driven. Community/faith-based is not explicitly mentioned.
low_level_thinking.Stakeholder focus: The paragraph emphasizes coordination and rationalization of financial efforts by government authorities and ministries, aligning with 'Government and policy makers'. It also mentions private initiative and reduced burden of apprenticeship, pointing to 'Private sector and industry'. 'Other' is included due to the mention of voluntary support and structural coordination, which don't fit neatly into other categories.
low_level_thinking.Cross-cutting themes & skills: The paragraph emphasizes financial rationalization of expenditures and coordination of education structures, aligning with 'Financial and economic literacy'. It mentions private initiative and reduced burden of apprenticeship, supporting 'Entrepreneurship and business skills'. The broader focus on education coordination and vocational specialization is captured by 'Other cross-cutting theme'. Categories like 'Digital and ICT literacy' and 'Sustainable development' are excluded as they are not explicitly mentioned.
low_level_thinking.Education orientation: The paragraph focuses on technical and vocational education (TVET) with specific mentions of coordination across education levels and financial rationalization. 'Combined general and vocational' is included due to references to structural coordination with primary, secondary, and higher education. 'General (academic)' is excluded as the emphasis is on TVET specialization and infrastructure costs, not general academic education.

TITLE: LE FINANCEMENT DE L’ÉDUCATION
paragraph: 21. The development of higher education (increase in the number of students, multiplication of specialized courses, needs for modern equipment in certain institutes or faculties, growing responsibilities in the field of scientific research, introduction of methods requiring the employment of a greater number of assistants, awarding of scholarships, etc.) demands ever larger appropriations; public authorities must therefore participate more extensively in its funding, without, however, compromising the autonomy towards which universities and higher education institutions in certain countries are increasingly striving.

low_level_thinking.Education level: The paragraph explicitly mentions 'higher education' and discusses funding, autonomy, and institutional requirements, aligning with 'Higher education unspecified'. 'Multiple levels' is included because the text references various aspects of higher education (e.g., scholarships, research, equipment) across different tiers. 'Curriculum' and 'Infrastructure & resources' are excluded as the focus is on funding and institutional autonomy, not specific curriculum elements or resource allocation details.
low_level_thinking.Ownership/Provision: The paragraph emphasizes public authorities' increasing participation in funding (Public) and collaboration without compromising autonomy, suggesting a partnership model (PPP). 'Private' categories are excluded as the text focuses on public involvement and institutional autonomy, not private entities.
low_level_thinking.Policy theme: The paragraph emphasizes public funding demands and institutional autonomy, aligning with 'Financing and resource mobilization' (funding needs) and 'Governance and planning' (autonomy and public authority involvement). 'Curriculum' and 'Infrastructure' were excluded as the focus is on funding mechanisms, not specific educational content or physical resources.
low_level_thinking.Stakeholder focus: The paragraph emphasizes public authorities' funding role (Government and policy makers), university autonomy and institutional responsibilities (Teachers and educators), and mentions students through references to scholarships and educational expansion (Learners and students). Civil society/NGOs, private sector, and international organizations are not explicitly mentioned.
low_level_thinking.Infrastructure & resources: The paragraph mentions 'modern equipment' and 'methods requiring the employment of a greater number of assistants', which align with 'Teaching and learning materials'. 'ICT and digital infrastructure' is implied by 'methods requiring assistants' and 'modern equipment' in academic settings. Other categories like 'School buildings' or 'Laboratories' are not explicitly mentioned, and 'Curriculum' is disputed in the summary.

TITLE: LE FINANCEMENT DE L’ÉDUCATION
paragraph: 22. The increase in primary school enrollments and the resulting shortage of primary school teachers, as well as the new demands for increasingly comprehensive professional training for teachers—impose new responsibilities on all countries, especially those that have not yet succeeded in generalizing compulsory education, in the field of teacher training; although the issue primarily concerns the training of primary education personnel, the new obligations arising from the professional preparation of staff for both secondary education and technical and vocational education must now be anticipated.

low_level_thinking.Education level: The paragraph explicitly mentions primary school enrollments and teacher shortages (ISCED 1), while also referencing secondary education and technical/vocational training (ISCED 2, 3). 'Multiple levels' is included due to the mention of both primary and secondary obligations. Pre-primary/early childhood categories are excluded as the text does not address these levels.
low_level_thinking.Teachers: The paragraph discusses teacher training obligations (Initial education and training), professional preparation for secondary and vocational education (Professional development), and shortages due to increased enrollments (Teacher supply and shortages). Legal status and rights are not explicitly mentioned, and other categories like recruitment or working conditions lack direct evidence.
low_level_thinking.Policy theme: The paragraph emphasizes teacher training obligations and systemic responsibilities (Governance and planning). It specifically mentions professional training for teachers and new responsibilities for countries, aligning with Teachers and workforce policies. Other categories like Curriculum and pedagogy policies are excluded as the focus is on training obligations, not curriculum content.
low_level_thinking.Stakeholder focus: The paragraph emphasizes new responsibilities on countries (government/policy makers) and teacher training obligations (teachers/educators). Learners, parents, civil society, etc., are not directly mentioned. Private sector and international organizations are not referenced.
low_level_thinking.Subject domain: The paragraph mentions 'technical and vocational education' directly, supporting 'Vocational and technical skills'. 'Social sciences and humanities' is implied through references to systemic responsibilities and policy themes related to education structures. Other categories like STEM or literacy are not explicitly mentioned.

TITLE: LE FINANCEMENT DE L’ÉDUCATION
paragraph: 23. Public authorities should provide financial support, both directly and through organizations operating in this field, for the establishment and development of adult education programs implemented either in the form of literacy campaigns or basic education to complement efforts to generalize compulsory education, or within the framework of plans for post-primary cultural extension, the importance of which will only increase as the number of working hours decreases.

low_level_thinking.Ownership/Provision: The paragraph explicitly mentions 'public authorities' providing financial support, indicating state-run ownership. 'Public-private partnership (PPP)' is excluded as there's no mention of private sector involvement. 'Community or faith-based' is not supported as the focus is on governmental institutions. The text does not reference non-profit or for-profit entities, ruling out those categories.
low_level_thinking.Policy theme: The paragraph emphasizes financial support from public authorities (Financing), adult education programs (Lifelong learning), and institutional frameworks for implementation (Governance). Categories like Access and equity or Quality are not explicitly mentioned, while International cooperation and Curriculum policies are not directly addressed.
low_level_thinking.Learner population: The paragraph explicitly mentions adult education programs and literacy campaigns, directly aligning with 'Adults and lifelong learners'. Financial support for these programs implies targeting low-income groups. Other categories like children or youth are excluded as the focus is on adults. 'Disadvantaged groups' is included due to the mention of financial support for development, suggesting targeted assistance.
low_level_thinking.Education level: The paragraph mentions adult education programs, literacy campaigns, basic education, and post-primary cultural extension, indicating multiple education levels. 'Basic education' and 'post-primary' suggest both primary and secondary levels, while 'adult education' implies non-compulsory levels. 'Multiple levels' captures this breadth without over-specifying.

TITLE: LE FINANCEMENT DE L’ÉDUCATION
paragraph: Special education (schools for children with disabilities, the blind, deaf-mutes, mentally retarded, physically handicapped, socially maladjusted, etc.) requires from the public authorities a specific financial effort and coordination between administrations and various public or private organizations concerned with this education.

low_level_thinking.Policy theme: The paragraph emphasizes financial effort and coordination between public and private entities, aligning with 'Financing and resource mobilization'. 'Governance and planning' fits due to the mention of coordination between administrations and organizations. Other categories like 'Inclusion and diversity' or 'Education orientation' are less directly supported by the text.
low_level_thinking.Ownership/Provision: The paragraph explicitly mentions 'public authorities' and coordination between 'public and private organizations', supporting 'Public (state-run)' and 'Public-private partnership (PPP)'. 'Private non-profit' and 'Private for-profit' are excluded as the paragraph does not specify private sector types. 'Community or faith-based' is not mentioned.

TITLE: LE FINANCEMENT DE L’ÉDUCATION
paragraph: Financing of Certain Categories of Expenditures 25. The unavoidable necessity of ensuring that teaching staff enjoy living conditions worthy of the mission entrusted to them, as well as the increased need for personnel at all levels of education, can only accentuate the upward trend in operating costs; even assuming that a certain rationalization of the structure of various types of education (secondary education and technical and vocational education, for example) might allow for savings, it remains the case that any hesitation in the financial effort to support recruitment and salaries of teaching staff only delays the resolution of the problem and makes it more difficult.

low_level_thinking.Teachers: The paragraph emphasizes the need for adequate living conditions and salaries for teaching staff, directly linking to 'Working conditions and salaries'. It also mentions increased personnel needs and delays in resolving staffing issues, supporting 'Teacher supply and shortages'. Other categories like recruitment/hiring are implied but not explicitly addressed, while professional development/qualification are not mentioned.
low_level_thinking.Policy theme: The paragraph emphasizes financial effort for teaching staff recruitment and salaries, directly linking to 'Financing and resource mobilization'. It also focuses on teaching staff conditions and personnel needs, aligning with 'Teachers and workforce policies'. Other categories like 'Governance and planning' or 'Infrastructure' are not explicitly mentioned.

TITLE: LE FINANCEMENT DE L’ÉDUCATION
paragraph: 26. The increase in student numbers at all levels, the new pedagogical, hygienic, and technical requirements concerning buildings and facilities, and the rising costs of materials and labor necessitate urgent measures based on carefully established statistical data, aimed, among other things, at: a) rationalizing methods and the use of materials for school construction; b) planning construction programs, where appropriate within the framework of long-term economic and social development plans; c) resorting to special funds and negotiating loans to accelerate the implementation of these programs; d) promoting assistance in kind or cash from private initiatives, etc.;

low_level_thinking.Policy theme: The paragraph emphasizes planning construction programs within long-term economic plans (Governance and planning) and mentions special funds, loans, and private assistance (Financing and resource mobilization). 'Access and equity' and 'Quality and learning outcomes' are excluded as the focus is on infrastructure planning and funding, not directly on access or learning outcomes.
low_level_thinking.Infrastructure & resources: The paragraph emphasizes school construction methods, materials, and planning (supporting 'School buildings and physical infrastructure'). It also mentions 'materials and labor' costs, aligning with 'Teaching and learning materials'. Other categories like ICT, WASH, or funding mechanisms are not explicitly addressed.

TITLE: LE FINANCEMENT DE L’ÉDUCATION
paragraph: 27. Public authorities should create or develop activities such as food and clothing assistance, the awarding of scholarships, travel opportunities, and holiday camps; it is also important that public authorities and interested private organizations coordinate their efforts in these various areas in order to ensure a rational allocation of resources.

low_level_thinking.Policy theme: The paragraph emphasizes coordination between public authorities and private organizations (Governance and planning) and rational allocation of resources (Financing and resource mobilization). 'Policy theme' is captured through governance structures, while 'resource allocation' aligns with financing. Other categories like 'International cooperation' are less directly supported.
low_level_thinking.Cross-cutting themes & skills: The paragraph emphasizes coordination and resource allocation, which aligns with 'Other cross-cutting theme' for broader thematic coordination. 'Life skills and 21st century competencies' fits as it includes activities like scholarships and holiday camps that develop practical skills. Categories like human rights or digital literacy are not explicitly mentioned, and the focus on public-private coordination supports the 'Other' category over more specific ones.

TITLE: LE FINANCEMENT DE L’ÉDUCATION
paragraph: Special modalities for financing education 28. The implementation of large-scale school construction programs, as well as the execution of extensive educational plans such as literacy and basic education campaigns, can be accelerated if, in addition to regular budgets, the responsible public authorities establish special funds or develop extraordinary budgets specifically allocated to the financing of these programs and plans;

low_level_thinking.Policy theme: The paragraph explicitly mentions special funds and extraordinary budgets for education programs, directly aligning with 'Financing and resource mobilization'. 'Governance and planning' is supported by references to public authorities establishing funds and executing educational plans. 'Infrastructure & resources' is excluded as the focus is on financing mechanisms, not physical construction.
low_level_thinking.Infrastructure & resources: The paragraph specifically mentions 'large-scale school construction programs' and 'educational plans' which directly relate to physical infrastructure and learning environments. 'Policy theme' and 'financing strategies' are excluded as they fall under the broader 'Infrastructure & resources' high-level label's policy aspect, but the paragraph focuses on implementation through construction and planning rather than budget mechanisms.

TITLE: LE FINANCEMENT DE L’ÉDUCATION
paragraph: 29. The development of financing plans extending beyond the annual fiscal year (such as five-year plans) appears necessary with regard to planning programs; staggered implementation over several years allows for the rationalization and coordinated distribution of efforts that cannot be expected with programs whose execution period does not exceed twelve months;

low_level_thinking.Policy theme: The paragraph emphasizes long-term financing plans and staggered implementation, aligning with 'Financing and resource mobilization'. It also mentions coordinated distribution of efforts and planning programs, supporting 'Governance and planning'. 'Policy theme' is excluded as the focus is on implementation mechanisms rather than overarching policy frameworks.
low_level_thinking.Education system monitoring & evaluation: The paragraph discusses long-term financing plans and staggered implementation for programs, which relates to systemic planning and resource allocation. This aligns with 'Education statistics and indicators' through data-driven planning and 'Quality assurance and accreditation' via coordinated distribution of efforts. 'Institutional evaluation and inspection' is excluded as the text lacks direct evaluation mechanisms.

TITLE: LE FINANCEMENT DE L’ÉDUCATION
paragraph: 30. It is advisable, when the situation permits, to resort to borrowing to finance investment expenditures (school construction, equipment, etc.); this system allows for the spreading of financial burdens over a large number of years and involves the younger generations who will benefit from them; while higher administrative authorities must authorize the borrowing and oversee the prudent use of the funds obtained, they must also facilitate the negotiation of this borrowing on the most favorable terms.

low_level_thinking.Infrastructure & resources: The paragraph explicitly mentions 'school construction' as a borrowing use case, directly linking to school buildings and physical infrastructure. Other categories like ICT or WASH are not mentioned. Policy aspects like authorization are excluded as per the summary's guidance to focus on concrete infrastructure references.
low_level_thinking.Policy theme: The paragraph discusses borrowing mechanisms for financing education infrastructure, aligning with 'Financing and resource mobilization'. It also mentions authorization, oversight, and facilitation of borrowing processes, which fall under 'Governance and planning'. Other categories like 'Infrastructure & resources' were excluded as the focus is on policy mechanisms rather than infrastructure itself.
low_level_thinking.Ownership/Provision: The paragraph discusses borrowing for school construction and equipment, which are public infrastructure projects. The mention of higher administrative authorities authorizing and overseeing borrowing aligns with public sector management. Private or PPP models are not explicitly mentioned, so other categories are excluded.
low_level_thinking.Legal frameworks: The paragraph discusses authorization, oversight, and facilitation of borrowing for educational infrastructure, aligning with 'Policy implementation and enforcement' through administrative oversight mechanisms. 'Rights and obligations of stakeholders' is relevant as it involves responsibilities of higher authorities and the involvement of future generations. 'Legal frameworks' is not directly addressed, focusing more on policy execution than legal structures.
low_level_thinking.Stakeholder focus: The paragraph emphasizes the role of higher administrative authorities in authorizing borrowing and overseeing fund use, which aligns with 'Government and policy makers'. Other categories like 'Parents and communities' or 'Private sector' are not mentioned. 'Infrastructure & resources' is implied but not a sub-label option here.

TITLE: LE FINANCEMENT DE L’ÉDUCATION
paragraph: 31. paragraph: 8. In the development of curricula for plastic arts and in the selection of corresponding methods, the objectives of this instruction must be constantly kept in mind, notably: a) its educational purposes (development of faculties of observation, imagination, expression, self-control, work discipline, spirit of collaboration in group work, etc.); b) its cultural purposes (education of taste, understanding and respect for works of art, etc.); c) its practical purposes (acquisition of techniques applicable in studies, in life, and in the profession); d) its orientation purposes (knowledge of the child’s psychology through their expressive attempts); e) its purposes of broad human understanding (universality of art, interest in and respect for the artistic manifestations of all peoples, great and small, training new generations in the spirit of humanity, friendship among peoples, and peace).

low_level_thinking.Curriculum: The paragraph focuses on curriculum design (development of curricula, selection of methods) and content (educational, cultural, practical purposes). It explicitly mentions learning outcomes like observation, imagination, and collaboration. 'Competency-based' aligns with skill development goals. 'Implementation' and 'evaluation' are not directly addressed, and 'other' is avoided due to specificity.
low_level_thinking.Pedagogy & assessment: The paragraph emphasizes educational objectives like observation, imagination, and self-control, aligning with pedagogical foundations. It also mentions 'orientation purposes' involving understanding child psychology, which ties to assessment and feedback mechanisms. 'Teaching methods and approaches' is excluded as methods are not explicitly discussed, and 'technology-supported pedagogy' is irrelevant here.
